runStatus: Manual March 30, 2026 snapshot from live Fed flow and official event schedules

coverageSummary.note: Only currencies with same-day market-relevant communication and tone-qualified wording are surfaced in the board.

prompt:
You are a macro & FX trading analyst specialized in real-time central bank communication tracking.

Your task is to identify ALL central bank communications TODAY (including live flow) for:
USD / Fed, EUR / ECB, JPY / BOJ, NZD / RBNZ, AUD / RBA, CHF / SNB, CAD / BoC, GBP / BoE

PRIMARY OBJECTIVE
Capture ALL MARKET-RELEVANT CENTRAL BANK FLOW, including:
- Speeches
- Interviews
- Panel discussions
- Q&A appearances
- Testimonies
- Meeting minutes / summaries
- Official statements
- Media appearances
- Press conferences
- Unscheduled remarks reported by news wires

CRITICAL SEARCH PRIORITY
1. Real-time news flow first
2. Official central bank sources second
3. Event-based sources third

TONE CLASSIFICATION
Tone must be exactly Hawkish, Neutral, or Dovish.
If there is not enough wording to defend a tone, omit the communication.

OUTPUT FORMAT
Date | Member | Role/Title | Communication Type | Status | Tone | Quote/Summary | Interpretation | Expected Impact

entries.quoteSummary: Reuters-cited reporting said Miran laid out a roadmap for a smaller Fed balance sheet alongside easier policy, arguing balance-sheet normalization need not tighten overall financial conditions if paired with changes that reduce structural reserve demand.
entries.interpretation: Dovish because he framed balance-sheet runoff as compatible with easier overall policy rather than tighter conditions. The read would turn less dovish if later remarks shift toward inflation persistence, reserve scarcity, or the need to keep policy restrictive.
entries.expectedImpact: USD bearish, weak to moderate, intraday to short-term.

entries.quoteSummary: Powell said the Fed can wait to see how the Iran war affects growth and inflation, arguing policymakers typically look through oil-price shocks and that policy is in a good place while inflation expectations remain anchored beyond the short term.
entries.interpretation: Dovish because he pushed back on pre-emptive hike fears, emphasized patience, and signaled energy-driven inflation alone is not yet enough to force tighter policy unless expectations start to drift higher.
entries.expectedImpact: USD bearish, moderate, intraday to short-term.

entries.bank: Federal Reserve Bank of New York
entries.roleTitle: President, Federal Reserve Bank of New York
entries.quoteSummary: Williams said inflation is near 3%, with tariffs adding 0.5 to 0.75 percentage points, but he still expects inflation to slow to about 2.75% this year and reach 2% in 2027, with no significant second-round tariff effects.
entries.interpretation: Neutral because he acknowledged near-term inflation pressure from tariffs and energy, but balanced it with expectations for disinflation, limited spillovers and a policy stance already well positioned for both mandates.
entries.expectedImpact: USD neutral to mildly bearish, weak, intraday to short-term.

sources.label: Economic Times article carrying Reuters-cited Miran remarks
sources.note: Used because no same-day official Fed transcript was available at snapshot time.

sources.label: Reuters via Investing.com on Powell's March 30 Harvard Q&A
sources.note: Same-day live coverage of Powell's remarks that the Fed can wait and see how the Iran war affects inflation and growth.

sources.label: Investing.com market summary of Williams' March 30 Staten Island remarks
sources.note: Same-day market summary capturing Williams' inflation, tariffs and policy-path remarks from Staten Island.
